claim: Explorer evidence is verified: YOLO demand evidence dispatch correctly implements multi-layer JSON repair with recoverable drift recording and fail-closed behavior for unrecoverable JSON via EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON, with PRD readiness remaining blocked during failures.

evidence.summary: parseJsonCandidate implements three-stage JSON repair: direct parse, regex-based repair of four Claude drift patterns, then error capture with repaired flag.
evidence.why: Verified core JSON repair logic handles recoverable drift patterns: line ranges, escape sequences, duplicate quotes, trailing commas.

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentResult returns EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON error code when parsed is null before provider failed check.
evidence.why: Verified fail-closed behavior for unrecoverable JSON triggers explicit error code.

evidence.summary: Provider run tracking exposes json_repaired boolean flag and parse_error truncation in result structure.
evidence.why: Verified that drift repair status is tracked and surfaced for diagnostics.

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime calls inspectDemandPrdReadiness, propagates blockers, and sets final result.status based on prd_ready flag.
evidence.why: Verified PRD readiness stays blocked when agents return errors, and final status reflects readiness.

evidence.summary: Test repairs minor Claude JSON drift and asserts json_repaired flag is set and result.status is pass.
evidence.why: Verified repair logic is tested for drift patterns with successful PRD readiness.

evidence.summary: Test validates invalid agent JSON produces EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON in agent_results and readiness.blockers with blocked status.
evidence.why: Verified fail-closed behavior is tested and enforced with explicit blocker codes.

evidence.summary: Boundary snapshot before/after detects target project mutations and reports BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers.
evidence.why: Verified boundary audit prevents evidence agents from modifying target project files.

risks: If JSON repair regex patterns are too permissive, they might incorrectly repair malicious or malformed output that should fail.
If Claude introduces new drift patterns beyond the four regex patterns, valid output could fail with EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON.
The dirty working tree state mentioned in scope_in could affect boundary snapshot behavior if uncommitted changes exist in evidence dispatch files.

result.notes: All explorer evidence verified against source code and tests. Implementation correctly handles recoverable JSON drift with explicit tracking and fails closed with EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON for unrecoverable cases. PRD readiness remains blocked during agent failures. Boundary audit detects target project mutations. Test coverage confirms success and failure paths. No assumptions promoted as facts.
